findings: All 10 prior phase files confirmed present: 01-product-owner.json through 10-technical-writer.json.
manifest.json: current_phase=11, scope=feature, expected_phases=[1..11], gate_events=11/44, halted=false, attempts={}.

QA (phase 7): 44/44 tests green in tests/keel-state-describe.test.cjs. Coverage ~93% (manual line mapping of cmdDescribe lines 507-609, 68/73 executable lines). Exceeds 80% gate. Path traversal LOW finding is pre-existing shared pattern across all commands — not introduced by this story.

AC traceability: AC-1 through AC-5 each mapped to passing tests in phases 7 and 8. AC-1 (9 assertions), AC-2 (3 assertions), AC-3 (6 assertions), AC-4 (4 assertions), AC-5 (22 regression assertions). Zero unaccounted ACs.

No open P0/P1 bugs identified within KEEL-103 scope. Pre-existing defect (test-keel-state.cjs final-gate regression from v3.13.0) is a known tracked issue — not introduced by this story and not blocking release of the describe feature.
decisions: Verdict: GO — subject to human PR creation and approval. All automated gates pass. No HIGH security findings. CHANGELOG and README updated. Zero blockers. 44/44 tests green at 93% coverage. All 5 ACs fully traced to passing assertions.

gitleaks findings are documentation placeholder strings (literal 'YOUR_TOKEN'), not real credentials. Both findings predate KEEL-103 by 6 days. Not counted as HIGH security findings for this release gate.

Pre-existing test failure in scripts/test-keel-state.cjs is a known regression from v3.13.0 scoping change. It is not caused by KEEL-103 and does not affect the describe command. It should be addressed as a separate story in the next sprint.
